# context-sync

> A Claude Code skill (MIT, shimo4228) that audits and fixes project documentation roles. Detects overlap between CLAUDE.md, docs/CODEMAPS/, docs/adr/, and README — enforces a four-role model so every concept lives in exactly one place. Phase 0 cascades to `codemap-writer` when CODEMAPS are stale, before any other phase runs.

## Core documentation

- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): self-contained Q&A reference for AI agents (Project Facts, Prior Research, question-form H2 sections)
- [README.md](README.md): human-facing narrative (why it matters, the problem, the four roles, real results)
- [SKILL.md](skills/context-sync/SKILL.md): full skill specification — 6-phase workflow, four-role model, Architecture's two surfaces, anti-patterns

## Architecture

- Four documentation roles: **Context** (how to work here — CLAUDE.md), **Architecture** (what the code looks like AND what concepts it defines — CODEMAPS prose + graph.jsonld triples), **Decisions** (why it's this way — ADR), **External** (what the project is — README)
- 6-phase workflow: Codemap Freshness Pre-check → Discover → Overlap Detection → Create / Migrate → Freshness Check → Report
- Architecture's two surfaces: prose (CODEMAPS for "where is X implemented?") + JSON-LD triples (graph.jsonld for "what is X / how does X relate to Y?")

## Standard reference

- [Agent Skill specification](https://agentskills.io/specification): the Claude Code skill format used by this repo
- [Answer.AI llms.txt standard](https://llmstxt.org/): the AI-facing documentation format this file implements

## Sibling skills

- [llms-txt-writer](https://github.com/shimo4228/llms-txt-writer): writes AI-facing docs optimized for ChatGPT / Perplexity / Gemini citation
- [skill-comply](https://github.com/shimo4228/skill-comply): measures whether agents actually follow skill / rule definitions (v0.3.0)
- [search-first](https://github.com/shimo4228/search-first): research-before-coding workflow
- [skill-stocktake](https://github.com/shimo4228/skill-stocktake): quality audit for skills and commands
- [rules-distill](https://github.com/shimo4228/rules-distill): scan installed skills, extract cross-cutting principles, and distill them into rules
- [learn-eval](https://github.com/shimo4228/learn-eval): extract reusable patterns from sessions, self-evaluate quality, and save to the right location
- [daily-research](https://github.com/shimo4228/daily-research): cron-driven daily research digest — theme selection, multi-stage web research, LLM-as-Judge evaluation
